name: Hydrogen 1S-2S two-photon transition
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the hydrogen 1S-2S transition frequency, the most precisely measured atomic transition. Parthey et al. 2011 measured f(1S-2S) = 2,466,061,413,187,035(10) Hz, fractional precision ~4.2 x 10^-15. SM prediction agrees within current proton-radius uncertainty.

regime.description: Atomic hydrogen, optical transition; sensitivity to QED vacuum polarization, nuclear structure, and any new electron-proton interaction.

current_bound.reference: Parthey et al., 'Improved Measurement of the Hydrogen 1S-2S Transition Frequency', PRL 107, 203001 (2011), arXiv:1107.3101

what_it_excludes: Any new long-range electron-proton interaction, modification of the Coulomb potential, or shift in QED vacuum-polarization corrections at the few-Hz level (i.e. fractional 10^-15) is excluded. Combined with hydrogen 2S-2P and muonic-hydrogen Lamb-shift measurements, sets the proton charge radius and tests bound-state QED at extreme precision.

parameterization.name: Bound-state QED with input parameters R_infinity, alpha, m_e, m_p, and proton charge radius r_p; plus optional new physics encoded as modifications to the e-p potential (Yukawa, dilaton, dark photon).
parameterization.reference: Beyer et al., Science 358, 79 (2017); CODATA recommended values; Karshenboim, PRD 73, 075024 (2006).
parameterization.summary: The 1S-2S frequency depends on these parameters with calculable QED corrections through alpha^7 m_e c^2 / h.

procedural.predicted_observable: f_1S2S = (3/4) c R_infinity * (1 + alpha^2 corrections + finite-nuclear-size corrections + QED radiative corrections).
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Any new e-p interaction with strength alpha' Yukawa range > 10^-10 m must satisfy |alpha' / alpha| < ~10^-10.
framework_inputs: If the framework modifies QED, predict the resulting shift in f_1S2S.
If the framework introduces new e-p interactions, compute the matrix element <1S-2S| V_new |1S-2S>.

primary_references.citation: Parthey et al., 'Improved Measurement of the Hydrogen 1S-2S Transition Frequency', PRL 107, 203001 (2011)
Matveev et al., 'Precision Measurement of the Hydrogen 1S-2S Frequency via a 920-km Fiber Link', PRL 110, 230801 (2013)
Beyer et al., 'The Rydberg constant and proton size from atomic hydrogen', Science 358, 79 (2017)
